title: Removed "x+", "x-", "y+", and "y-" from footprintInsertionDirection
description: Removes the directional options x, x-, y, and y- from the footprint insertion direction type definition, streamlining the available options for users.

title: Refactor CAD offset logic to use model bounds + SVG origin extraction
description: This pull request refactors the CAD offset logic to utilize model bounds and extract the SVG origin. It introduces a new method for calculating the CAD model offset based on the bounds of the model, improving the accuracy of the placement of CAD models in the circuit design. The changes include updates to the conversion functions and adjustments to the handling of CAD model properties, ensuring that the models origin is correctly calculated and applied during the conversion process.

title: Infer internal footprint connections for split pins and shared aliases
description: This change makes repeated non-overlapping footprint contacts behave like implicit internally connected pins instead of ambiguous PCB targets, allowing traces to target shared aliases directly and updating port matching accordingly.
body:
This makes repeated non-overlapping footprint contacts behave like implicit internally connected pins instead of ambiguous PCB targets. Separate physical contact clusters now become separate source ports with one PCB port each, and they are tied together through existing internal-connection machinery with no schema change. Shared non-numeric aliases like A1 also infer the same internal connection, so traces can target the alias directly.

  It also updates pad and plated-hole matching to assign each primitive to the correct inferred port, removes the old ambiguous-port expectations from imported footprint tests, and adds repro coverage with PCB SVG snapshots for split-pin and battery clip cases.

title: chore: update packages @tscircuit/checks
description: Updates the tscircuitchecks package from version 0.0.119 to 0.0.120 in the package.json file.

title: Update props/circuit-json versions and migrate board spacing fields to clearance
description: Updates DRC properties in the board schema from spacing to clearance to align with upstream package changes and resolves TypeScript errors.
body:
### Motivation
- Upstream packages renamed DRC properties from `spacing` to `clearance`, causing TypeScript errors and mismatches between parsed props and `circuit-json` types. 
- The board DRC schema now exposes `min_board_edge_clearance` in the updated JLC tolerances and it should be propagated into inserted `PcbBoard` objects.

### Description
- Bumped dependencies in `package.json`: `@tscircuit/props` → `^0.0.510`, `circuit-json` → `^0.0.418`, and updated `@tscircuit/jlcpcb-manufacturing-specs` to commit `5644e294acfdd545ceb468c3b95c9ed82cb9a00b`.
- Replaced board field mappings in `lib/components/normal-components/Board.ts` from `min_trace_to_pad_spacing`/`min_pad_to_pad_spacing` to `min_trace_to_pad_clearance`/`min_pad_to_pad_clearance` and added `min_board_edge_clearance` sourced from subcircuit props or `jlcMinTolerances`.
- Updated the test `tests/components/normal-components/board-manufacture-drc-property.test.tsx` to use the new prop names `minTraceToPadClearance`/`minPadToPadClearance` and assert the corresponding PCB board fields.

### Testing
- Ran typecheck with `bunx tsc --noEmit` which completed successfully.
- Ran the updated test with `bun test tests/components/normal-components/board-manufacture-drc-property.test.tsx` and it passed (1 test, 0 failures).

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_69e7bd4745d083279464709e3e8f0652)

title: update footprinter dep
description: Updates the version of the tscircuitfootprinter dependency from 0.0.349 to 0.0.351 in package.json

title: Use jlcMinTolerances values for DRC defaults
description: Aligns local DRC default values with the published JLCPCB minimum manufacturing tolerances, replacing hardcoded values with dynamic values from the jlcMinTolerances package.
body:
### Motivation
- Align local DRC default values with the published JLCPCB minimum manufacturing tolerances so checks use the manufacturer minimums rather than hardcoded values.

### Description
- Add `@tscircuit/jlcpcb-manufacturing-specs` to `devDependencies` pinned to commit `fa54ff1f9eabca1b852a0b035db907061e5c4a2f` in `package.json`.
- Import `jlcMinTolerances` from `@tscircuit/jlcpcb-manufacturing-specs` in `lib/drc-defaults.ts` and use its fields as the source for DRC minimums.
- Replace hardcoded defaults with expressions that prefer `jlcMinTolerances` values and fall back to the previous numeric constants where the package does not supply a value.
- Preserve unrelated constants like `DEFAULT_VIA_BOARD_MARGIN` and `EPSILON` unchanged.

### Testing
- Ran `bun run format`, which completed but reported existing large-file warnings for JSON fixtures; formatting run finished (warnings only).
- Performed `npm install` (git dependency resolved) and `npm run build`, and the build completed successfully.

------
[Codex Task](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/tasks/task_b_69e7ca0129288327999f5467f29be306)

title: Remove unused local BomTable component
description: Removes the local BomTable component and its associated logic as it is no longer referenced in the application, streamlining the codebase without affecting runtime behavior.
body:
This PR deletes src/components/BomTable.tsx, which is no longer referenced anywhere in the app. The BOM view uses BomTable from @tscircuit/runframe, so removing the local duplicate does not change runtime
  behavior.

Removals:

* Deleted the `BomTable` React component, which rendered a bill of materials table for source components in a circuit JSON.
* Removed the `linkify` helper function that generated supplier-specific links for part numbers.
* Eliminated related imports, interface definitions, and filtering/mapping logic for supplier columns and source components.

title: feat: include supplier part number in KiCad footprint properties

body: This PR enables the inclusion of supplier part numbers (specifically from `jlcpcb`) as properties in the generated KiCad PCB footprints. ### Key Changes - **Metadata Extraction**: Updated `getKicadComponentProperty` to extract supplier part numbers from the `supplier_part_numbers` record in `source_component`. - **Footprint Property Injection**: Modified `applyMetadataToFootprint` to inject the extracted supplier part number as a [(property "Supplier Part Number" "...")] field in the KiCad footprint definition

title: fix: preserve source pin identity in KiCad pad numbers (#212)
description: Fixes KiCad pad labeling to preserve source pin identity instead of array order, ensuring correct pad numbering for custom footprints.
body:
## Summary

- KiCad export was labelling pads by `pcb_smtpad` / `pcb_plated_hole` array order instead of by source pin identity, so a custom footprint declared in reverse physical order (`portHints={["pin4"]}` at x=-3, ..., `portHints={["pin1"]}` at x=3) came out with inverted KiCad pad labels.
- Added `getKiCadPadNumber` — walks `pcb_port → source_port.pin_number`, with fallbacks to pin-like `port_hints` (`"pin4"` → `"4"`, BGA-style `"A1"` → `"A1"`) and the existing sequential counter as last resort.
- Threaded the resolver through `convertSmdPads` / `convertPlatedHoles` as an optional `getPadNumber` callback, mirroring the existing `getNetInfo` pattern in `AddFootprintsStage`.

Fixes #212.

## Before vs after

Same four-pad footprint from the issue repro (`portHints` in reverse order):

| pad position `(at x y)` | before | after |
|---|---|---|
| `-3 0` | `pad "1"` | `pad "4"` |
| `-1 0` | `pad "2"` | `pad "3"` |
| ` 1 0` | `pad "3"` | `pad "2"` |
| ` 3 0` | `pad "4"` | `pad "1"` |

## Notes

- The pad UUID seed includes `padNumber` (`pad:${componentId}:${padNumber}:${x},${y}`), so UUIDs for affected pads will change once. This is desirable — UUIDs now track pin identity rather than array order, so reordering pads in source no longer churns them.
- `getPadNumber` is optional on both converter functions to keep the signatures backward-compatible for any external callers.
- Fallback counter keeps incrementing every iteration so unresolved pads on the sequence-number fallback path don't collide with each other.

## Test plan

- [x] New regression test `tests/pcb/repros/repro13-custom-footprint-pad-numbering.test.tsx` — exact repro from the issue, asserts pad `"4"` is at x=-3, pad `"1"` at x=3, etc.
- [x] `bun run typecheck` clean
- [x] `bun run format:check` clean
- [ ] CI green (including PNG snapshot tests that need `kicad-cli`)

title: Polish mobile feature cards and footer layout
description: Enhances the small-screen experience on the landing page by tightening the feature card layout, simplifying repeated card styles, and redesigning the footer for mobile to read as a compact sitemap instead of a squeezed desktop stack.
body:
Improve the small-screen experience on the landing page by tightening the feature card layout, simplifying repeated card styles, and redesigning the footer for mobile so it reads as a compact, intentional
  sitemap instead of a squeezed desktop stack.

  Changes:

  - Simplify feature card CSS and remove repeated inline styling
  - Center and constrain feature cards on very small screens
  - Convert the mobile footer into compact link-group cards
  - Improve spacing and hierarchy in the footer brand and note area

  Validation:

  - Visual/layout change only
  - No automated tests added

/closes https://github.com/tscircuit/tscircuit.com/issues/3220

title: Replace autorouting_example.mp4 with autorouting_animation.mov; update video source and styles for better presentation
description: Updates the landing page with improved UI elements, navigation simplification, animated GitHub star count, and replaces the autorouting demo video.
body:
This pull request updates the `index.html` file with several improvements to the UI, navigation, and user experience, focusing on enhanced feature descriptions, improved GitHub star count display, and minor visual tweaks. The most important changes are grouped below.

### UI and Feature Description Improvements

* Updated feature descriptions for AI coding skills, open source/forkability, and analog simulation to include clearer language and relevant links [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1463-R1475) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1502-R1507) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1513-R1522).
* Added inline background colors to preview images for PCB and schematic panels for better visual clarity.
* Added a "Quick Start" button linking to the CLI quickstart guide for easier onboarding.

### Navigation Simplification

* Removed the redundant "Product" link from both the desktop and mobile navigation menus to streamline navigation [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1279) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1302).

### GitHub Star Count Animation and Fallback

* Implemented animated counting for the GitHub star count, including improved formatting and a fallback value for better user engagement and resilience to API failures [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051R1844-R1847) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1962-R2033) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1987-R2062) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051R2322-R2325).

### Minor Visual and Media Updates

* Changed the `object-fit` CSS property from `cover` to `fit` for media elements, and updated the autorouting demo video and its container styling [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L375-R375) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-0eb547304658805aad788d320f10bf1f292797b5e6d745a3bf617584da017051L1463-R1475).

These changes collectively improve the clarity, interactivity, and visual polish of the landing page.
